svg.title: Compounding context vs REM bounded loop
svg.desc: Top panel shows a naive agent loop where each step's output is appended to the next input, so context grows until the window overflows. Bottom panel shows the REM loop where the NPU compactor folds raw turns into a memory store and a selector injects a small bounded slice, keeping per-step context flat.
svg.text: A. naive loop — output re-enters input
B. REM loop — injected context stays flat
